body_txt: In a crisis, nobody wants to feel useless or left out. Some people may want to run away, but otherwise they'll usually try to help. The problem is, sometimes this help turns out to be rather unhelpful. Depending on the situation, it can be anywhere from irritating to downright dangerous, for the "helper" and the "helpee". As such, this situation can have several different outcomes, ranging from simple annoyance on the part of the people being inconvenienced to extremely dire consequences that involve a lot of people getting killed . Compare Chronic Hero Syndrome , Unwanted Rescue , Tragically Misguided Favor , Complaining About Rescues They Don't Like , The Load , The Millstone , Ungrateful Bastard . Especially self-righteous and/or stubborn "helpers" may display Condescending Compassion . In a more modern setting, this can be a way to play a Benevolent A.I. for laughs. Contrast Shut Up and Save Me! . Can lead to Always Need What You Gave Up if the person ends up in a position where the aid of the person they ordered not to help is needed . May overlap with Overzealous Underling . Involuntary Cheating will often be considered this to someone who has a sense of fair play. For "helpers" provided by a program that annoy or otherwise have a negative effect on the player, see Annoying Video Game Helper .
